text: The structural evolution and luminescence enhancement of (Sr,Ca)AlSiN3:Eu2+ phosphors induced by different cation substitution modes were investigated systemically. Upon replacing Ca2+ with larger Sr2+ ions, the (Sr,Ca)AlSiN3 host lattice undergoes selective anisotropic expansion, which results in the increase in lattice volume and b-c plane dimensions, and the simultaneous decrease in interlayer distance along the a-axis. The structural evolution leads to the distortion of the AlSiN3 framework, resulting in high-coordinate Sr/Eu sites. The Eu2+ charge states in the (Sr,Ca)AlSiN3:Eu2+ phosphors were characterized via X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) spectroscopy and are dependent on the substitution mode and the atmosphere used in synthesis. Accordingly, the Eu2+ emission shows a blue shift in position and the photoluminescence intensity of (Sr,Ca)AlSiN3:Eu2+ increases by over 10% by using different substitution modes. A clear relationship between the macroscopic lattice parameters and the local structure of Sr/Eu sites and the corresponding Eu2+–ex band positions has been established for the nitride phosphors, which is very helpful for designing highly efficient wLEDs.
